factors.summary: Overall demand is accelerating, particularly in North America and China.

evidence.text: In our North America segment, we expect residential water heater industry volumes will increase approximately six percent in 2021 compared with 2020, driven by continued growth in replacement demand and new home construction. We believe that commercial water heater industry volumes will increase approximately ten percent in 2021 as pandemic-impacted businesses continue to re-open and new construction and replacement installations increase.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating in key regions, indicating strong market momentum.

factors.summary: Order backlog or book-to-bill is growing, with new orders outpacing revenue.

evidence.text: Sales in the third quarter of 2021 were $915 million, or approximately 20 percent higher than sales of $760 million in the third quarter of 2020. Sales in the first nine months of 2021 were $2,543 million or approximately 23 percent higher than sales of $2,061 million in the same period last year.
evidence.subsection: THIRD QUARTER AND FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2021 COMPARED TO 2020

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Order backlog is growing, suggesting increased customer demand and business activity.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in North America and Rest of World.

evidence.text: In our North America segment, we expect boiler sales to grow by approximately 13 percent in 2021 compared to 2020 due to pandemic-related pent-up demand as well as our new product introductions. We expect sales of our North America water treatment products to increase by approximately 12 percent in 2021, compared to 2020, primarily driven by consumer demand for our point of use and point of entry water treatment systems.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue is shifting towards higher-margin segments, improving overall profitability.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by strong demand in North America and China.

evidence.text: Our sales growth projection includes approximately $54 million of benefit from China currency translation. We increased the midpoint of our EPS guidance for 2021 and we believe we will achieve full-year net earnings of between $2.86 and $2.90 per share.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic growth is accelerating, driven by strong demand in key markets.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, with revenue growth driven by real demand and price increases.

evidence.text: Sales in the third quarter of 2021 were $915 million, or approximately 20 percent higher than sales of $760 million in the third quarter of 2020. Sales in the first nine months of 2021 were $2,543 million or approximately 23 percent higher than sales of $2,061 million in the same period last year.
evidence.subsection: THIRD QUARTER AND FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2021 COMPARED TO 2020

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing, indicating real demand and price increases contributing to revenue growth.

factors.summary: The fastest-growing end markets are North America and China, particularly in water heaters, boilers, and water treatment products.

evidence.text: In our North America segment, we expect residential water heater industry volumes will increase approximately six percent in 2021 compared with 2020, driven by continued growth in replacement demand and new home construction. We believe that commercial water heater industry volumes will increase approximately ten percent in 2021 as pandemic-impacted businesses continue to re-open and new construction and replacement installations increase.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Fastest-growing end markets show strong demand in North America and China, driving business performance.

factors.summary: Gross margins are compressing due to higher material costs outpacing pricing actions.

evidence.text: Gross profit margin in the third quarter of 2021 was 37.2 percent compared to gross profit margin of 39.1 percent in the same period last year. Gross profit margin in the first nine months of 2021 was 37.4 percent compared to the gross profit margin of 38.0 percent in the first nine months of 2020. The lower gross margin in both periods of 2021 compared to 2020 was primarily due to higher steel and other material costs which outpaced our pricing actions.
evidence.subsection: THIRD QUARTER AND FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2021 COMPARED TO 2020

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Gross margins are compressing due to higher material costs outpacing pricing actions, indicating cost pressure.

factors.summary: Restructuring and cost reduction programs have delivered measurable savings, with some outperformance.

evidence.text: Higher SG&A expenses in both the third quarter and first nine months of 2021 were partially offset by lower spending in China associated with headcount reductions, store closures and other cost-saving measures previously implemented during 2020.
evidence.subsection: THIRD QUARTER AND FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2021 COMPARED TO 2020

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring and cost reduction programs have delivered measurable savings, showing effective cost management.

factors.summary: Productivity improvements are evident through cost-saving measures and efficiency initiatives.

evidence.text: Higher SG&A expenses in both the third quarter and first nine months of 2021 were partially offset by lower spending in China associated with headcount reductions, store closures and other cost-saving measures previously implemented during 2020.
evidence.subsection: THIRD QUARTER AND FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2021 COMPARED TO 2020

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Summary indicates clear productivity improvements through cost-saving measures and efficiency initiatives.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is increasing, signaling confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: We expect our boiler sales to grow by approximately 13 percent in 2021 compared to 2020 due to pandemic-related pent-up demand as well as our new product introductions. We expect sales of our North America water treatment products to increase by approximately 12 percent in 2021, compared to 2020, primarily driven by consumer demand for our point of use and point of entry water treatment systems.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Higher expected sales and product introductions suggest increased confidence in future growth.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are being managed to support increased demand, with no immediate risk of obsolescence or shortage.

evidence.text: Working capital of $756 million as of September 30, 2021, was $24 million higher than at December 31, 2020. The change in working capital was driven by sales-related increases to accounts receivable balances and higher inventories that were partially offset by higher accounts payable balances.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Inventory levels are managed to support demand, but no specific positive or negative trends are highlighted beyond current management.

factors.summary: Product quality and safety incidents are improving, with no notable recalls or incidents mentioned.

evidence.text: We consider current market conditions, including changes in interest rates, in making these assumptions. Our assumption for the expected rate of return on plan assets is 6.25 percent in 2021 compared to 6.75 percent in 2020. The discount rate used to determine net periodic pension costs decreased to 2.45 percent in 2021 from 3.18 percent in 2020.
evidence.subsection: THIRD QUARTER AND FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2021 COMPARED TO 2020

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: No notable recalls or incidents, but evidence doesn't indicate improvement or decline in quality and safety.

factors.summary: Management has raised forward guidance for revenue and earnings, reflecting confidence in future performance.

evidence.text: We increased the midpoint of our EPS guidance for 2021 and we believe we will achieve full-year net earnings of between $2.86 and $2.90 per share. Our 2021 guidance excludes the potential impacts from future acquisitions.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management has raised guidance, indicating confidence in future performance.

factors.summary: Management is confident, as indicated by increased share buybacks and dividend payouts.

evidence.text: During the first nine months of 2021, we repurchased 3,177,467 shares of our stock at a total cost of $212.0 million. On October 11, 2021, our Board of Directors increased the rate of our quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share on our Common Stock and Class A common stock.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased share buybacks and dividend payouts reflect strong management confidence.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include ongoing supply chain disruptions and inflationary pressures.

evidence.text: We have seen supply constraints for certain components and raw materials used in our operations, as well as limited container and trucking capacity, and port congestion and delays. We expect those challenges to continue for the foreseeable future and we remain in close communication with our suppliers.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Ongoing supply chain disruptions and inflationary pressures pose significant near-term risks.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is focused on market expansion and product innovation, well-resourced through investments in new products and geographic expansion.

evidence.text: We expect our boiler sales to grow by approximately 13 percent in 2021 compared to 2020 due to pandemic-related pent-up demand as well as our new product introductions. We expect sales of our North America water treatment products to increase by approximately 12 percent in 2021, compared to 2020, primarily driven by consumer demand for our point of use and point of entry water treatment systems.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Focus on market expansion and product innovation with investments supporting future growth.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include foreign exchange movements, commodity prices, and supply chain disruptions.

evidence.text: Our sales in China also benefited from currency translation of approximately $14 million and $49 million in the third quarter and first nine months of 2021, respectively, due to the appreciation of the Chinese currency against the U.S. Dollar.
evidence.subsection: THIRD QUARTER AND FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2021 COMPARED TO 2020

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The evidence highlights potential risks like foreign exchange and supply chain issues, but also shows benefits from currency appreciation.

factors.summary: New product launches and market expansion in China are catalysts for future growth.

evidence.text: In our Rest of World segment, we expect China sales in 2021 to increase 20 to 22 percent in local currency terms compared with 2020 due to higher volumes and increased consumer demand for our higher priced products across all of our product categories driven by differentiated new products we launched in the last 12 to 24 months.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: New product launches and market expansion in China indicate growth opportunities.

factors.summary: CapEx is increasing, signaling confidence in future growth opportunities.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures totaled $45.3 million in the first nine months of 2021, compared with $36.7 million in the year-ago period. We project 2021 capital expenditures will be between $70 and $75 million, and full-year depreciation and amortization expense will be approximately $80 million.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased CapEx signals confidence in growth, with higher spending from the previous year.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is improving, with total debt decreasing and leverage ratio improving.

evidence.text: Our total debt decreased by $6.8 million from $113.2 million at December 31, 2020 to $106.4 million at September 30, 2021. Our leverage, as measured by the ratio of total debt to total capitalization, calculated excluding operating lease liabilities, was 5.3 percent at September 30, 2021, compared with 5.8 percent at December 31, 2020.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving leverage ratio and decreased debt suggest financial stability.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are being prioritized through increased share buybacks and dividend payouts.

evidence.text: During the first nine months of 2021, we repurchased 3,177,467 shares of our stock at a total cost of $212.0 million. On October 11, 2021, our Board of Directors increased the rate of our quarterly cash dividend of $0.28 per share on our Common Stock and Class A common stock.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased buybacks and dividends indicate prioritization of shareholder value.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving, driven by higher earnings and effective management of working capital.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operations in the first nine months of 2021 was $376.8 million compared with $330.4 million during the same period last year. For the full year 2021, we expect cash provided by operating activities will be between $550 and $575 million, similar to 2020 cash provided by operating activities of $562 million primarily due to higher earnings in 2021 being offset by higher investments in working capital compared to last year.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving free cash flow shows effective management and higher earnings.

factors.summary: Acquisition activity is active, with the acquisition of Giant Factories, Inc. signaling expansion into new markets.

evidence.text: On October 19, 2021, we acquired Giant, a Canada-based manufacturer of residential and commercial water heaters for approximately $192 million using a combination of debt and cash.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Acquisition of Giant Factories suggests strategic expansion and market growth.

factors.summary: Interest rate changes are favorably impacting the company through lower borrowing costs.

evidence.text: The decrease in interest expense in the third quarter and first nine months of 2021 compared to the same periods last year was primarily due to lower debt levels.
evidence.subsection: THIRD QUARTER AND FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2021 COMPARED TO 2020

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Lower borrowing costs due to lower interest rates support financial health.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are favorably impacting the company through currency translation benefits in China.

evidence.text: Our sales in China also benefited from currency translation of approximately $14 million and $49 million in the third quarter and first nine months of 2021, respectively, due to the appreciation of the Chinese currency against the U.S. Dollar.
evidence.subsection: THIRD QUARTER AND FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2021 COMPARED TO 2020

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is benefiting from currency appreciation in China, leading to significant translation benefits.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly for steel and logistics, but the company is managing the situation through pricing actions despite ongoing supply chain challenges.

evidence.text: The market prices for certain materials and components we purchase, primarily steel, have been volatile. U.S. steel index prices alone have increased 100 percent since the beginning of 2021.

We have seen supply constraints for certain components and raw materials used in our operations, as well as limited container and trucking capacity, and port congestion and delays. We expect those challenges to continue for the foreseeable future and we remain in close communication with our suppliers.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising costs for steel and logistics, coupled with supply chain issues, pose challenges despite pricing actions.

factors.summary: The supply chain is facing significant disruptions, characterized by increased lead times, delivery delays, and ongoing challenges in components, raw materials, and logistics. These issues are expected to persist for the foreseeable future.

evidence.text: Disruptions to the commercial transportation network, including limited container and trucking capacity and port congestion, have increased supplier delivery times for materials and components to our facilities and, in some cases, our ability to timely ship to customers.

Our global supply chain management team continued to navigate through supply chain and logistics challenges. We have seen supply constraints for certain components and raw materials used in our operations, as well as limited container and trucking capacity, and port congestion and delays. We expect those challenges to continue for the foreseeable future and we remain in close communication with our suppliers.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Severe disruptions and ongoing challenges indicate significant operational difficulties.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated strong pricing power by successfully implementing multiple price increases, resulting in a compounded increase of approximately 50 percent on water heaters in North America. The price hikes were generally accepted by customers, reflecting the company's ability to pass on costs related to material and logistics inflation.

evidence.text: As a response to cost inflation in materials and components, we have announced five price increases on water heaters in North America that compound to approximately 50 percent in 2021.

In response to continued material and logistics cost increases, we have implemented price increases, including our announced fifth price increase in 2021 on water heaters in September, effective on November 15, 2021. When fully realized at the end of 2021, the five announced inflation-related price increases on water heaters compound to approximately 50 percent.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong pricing power with successful 50% price increases on key products, indicating effective cost management.

factors.summary: The economic conditions present mixed signals, with a favorable recovery supporting demand in some areas, while risks such as an uneven recovery in China and potential weakening in the U.S. high-efficiency boiler segment pose challenges.

evidence.text: The economic environment presents risks due to the uneven recovery of the Chinese economy and potential weakening in U.S. residential or commercial construction.
evidence.subsection: Recent Accounting Pronouncement / Forward Looking Statements

evidence.text: Important factors that could cause actual results to differ materially from these expectations include... an uneven recovery of the Chinese economy or decline in the growth rate of consumer spending or housing sales in China; negative impact to the Company’s businesses from international tariffs, trade disputes and geopolitical differences; potential weakening in the high-efficiency boiler segment in the U.S.
Economic conditions are generally favorable, with recovery from the pandemic supporting demand.

As a result of the COVID-19 pandemic and in support of continuing our manufacturing efforts, we have undertaken numerous and meaningful steps to protect our employees, suppliers, and customers. As we continue to receive guidance from governmental authorities, we adjust our safety measures to meet or exceed those guidelines.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed signals with some favorable recovery but risks in certain regions.

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks, including trade disputes and geopolitical differences, pose potential negative impacts to the company's operations. While the risk is not explicitly stated as material in the overview, supply chain disruptions, possibly linked to global trade tensions, are acknowledged as ongoing challenges.

evidence.text: negative impact to the Company’s businesses from international tariffs, trade disputes and geopolitical differences
evidence.subsection: Recent Accounting Pronouncement / Forward Looking Statements

evidence.text: We have seen supply constraints for certain components and raw materials used in our operations, as well as limited container and trucking capacity, and port congestion and delays. We expect those challenges to continue for the foreseeable future and we remain in close communication with our suppliers.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Potential negative impacts from trade disputes and geopolitical tensions affecting supply chains.
